Operation 'language-detection' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_context. Original summary 'Language Detection' is too generic and not action-oriented. Description lacks clarity on input format (array of texts), output structure, and practical use cases. Added details about: (1) what the operation accepts (array of text strings), (2) what it returns (language_code for each input), (3) the constraint about supported languages, and (4) practical context for AI agents to understand when to use this operation. Improved terminology consistency with the API's psycholinguistic analytics domain.

data.current_description:
Identifies what language a text is written in. Only languages that our API supports can be analyzed.

Returned labels:
* language_code of the detected language
data.description_suggestion: Detects the language of provided text input and returns the identified language code. Supports a limited set of languages - only languages in the API's supported language list can be accurately detected. Accepts an array of text strings in the request body and returns language detection results for each input, including the detected language_code. Useful for preprocessing multilingual content or routing text to language-specific analysis pipelines.
data.reasoning: Original summary 'Language Detection' is too generic and not action-oriented. Description lacks clarity on input format (array of texts), output structure, and practical use cases. Added details about: (1) what the operation accepts (array of text strings), (2) what it returns (language_code for each input), (3) the constraint about supported languages, and (4) practical context for AI agents to understand when to use this operation. Improved terminology consistency with the API's psycholinguistic analytics domain.

data.current_description:
Identify the purpose and writing style of a written text.

 Supported Languages: [`ar`, `de`, `en`, `es`, `fr`, `it`, `nl`, `pt`, `ru`, `tr`, `zh`]

Returned labels:
* action-seeking
* fact-oriented
* information-seeking
 * self-revealing
data.summary_suggestion: Analyze text communication purpose and writing style
data.description_suggestion: Analyzes written text to identify its communication purpose and writing style. Classifies text into categories including action-seeking, fact-oriented, information-seeking, and self-revealing. Supports 11 languages: Arabic, German, English, Spanish, French, Italian, Dutch, Portuguese, Russian, Turkish, and Chinese. Returns classification labels with confidence scores indicating the detected communication patterns.
data.reasoning: Original summary 'Communication & Tonality' is too vague and uses ampersand notation. Description lacks clarity about what is returned and how results are structured. Added specific output details about classification categories, language support clarity, and return value structure for better AI agent interpretability.

Detect the emotions of the text.

Returned labels:

* joy
* love
* sadness
* surprise

data.description_suggestion: Detects and classifies emotions expressed in text using a standard emotion model. Returns emotion classification labels with confidence scores for each detected emotion category.

data.current_description:

Supported Langauges: [`en`, `de`, `es`]

Returned labels:
* anger
* disgust
* fear
* joy
 * sadness
* surprise
* no-emotion
data.summary_suggestion: Analyze text emotions using Ekman model
data.description_suggestion: Detects and classifies emotions in text using Ekman's seven-emotion model. Identifies anger, disgust, fear, joy, sadness, surprise, and no-emotion states. Supports three languages: English, German, and Spanish. Returns emotion classification labels with confidence scores for each Ekman emotion category, enabling psycholinguistic analysis of emotional expression.

data.current_description:
Predict the personality trait of author of any written text.
 
Supported Languages: [`ar`, `de`, `en`, `es`, `fr`, `it`, `nl`, `pt`, `ru`, `tr`, `zh`]

Returned labels:

* emotional
* rational
data.description_suggestion: Predicts the personality traits of a text's author by analyzing writing patterns and linguistic markers. Classifies authors as either emotional or rational personality types based on their written expression. Supports 11 languages: Arabic, German, English, Spanish, French, Italian, Dutch, Portuguese, Russian, Turkish, and Chinese. Returns personality classification labels with confidence scores indicating the detected personality dimension.
data.reasoning: Summary is clear and actionable. Description needs enhancement with output details and clarity about what personality dimensions are measured. Added specificity about how predictions are made, what the two personality types represent, and how results are returned for better AI agent understanding.

data.current_summary: Predicts whether a phrase is a promoter, detractor or indifferent
data.summary_suggestion: Classify customer sentiment as promoter, detractor, or neutral
data.description_suggestion: Classifies customer feedback or brand-related text into Net Promoter Score (NPS) categories: promoter (positive brand advocate), detractor (dissatisfied customer), or indifferent (neutral sentiment). Analyzes text to predict customer loyalty and satisfaction levels. Accepts text input and returns classification labels indicating the customer sentiment category. Useful for brand health monitoring and customer satisfaction analysis.
